_comment: Person template — copy into content/people/<person-slug>.json. The file name should equal the id (validator warns on mismatch). Keys starting with _ are authoring comments; the validator strips them.
_id: kebab-case ascii slug, unique across ALL entity types, e.g. "david-ben-gurion".
_bio: Short Hebrew biography, 1-3 sentences for the detail panel.

bio.he: ביוגרפיה קצרה של האישיות, משפט אחד עד שלושה.

_lifespan: Dates use "YYYY" | "YYYY-MM" | "YYYY-MM-DD". "end": null means still alive — rendered as an open-ended lifespan. Use only the precision you actually know.
_categoryIds: At least one id from content/taxonomies/person-categories.json — the first category drives the item color.
_importance: Integer 1-100 per the docs/05 rubric: 90-100 era-defining figures; 70-89 major national figures; 40-69 central figures in their field; 20-39 cultural/contextual figures (year-level zoom); 1-19 fine detail (month-level zoom).
_regionIds: ids from content/taxonomies/regions.json the person is associated with.
_links: Optional related reading (further links). NOT the citation — use "sources". Placeholder URLs ("...", example.com) are rejected.
_sources: REQUIRED — at least one citation backing the facts (docs/04#sourcing). Prefer an authoritative institution; add "url" only when it is a real, stable page. "publisher"/"kind" optional (archive|library|museum|encyclopedia|reference|academic|government|book|press|website).
